mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5 percent, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 70 percent of observations when INDPRO declines during tightening periods, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or the threshold effect disappears in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS levels falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no directional bias during payroll expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative Rank IC below -0.02, or 20-day returns are positive in over 65 percent of synchronized decline observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above the 100 index threshold signals robust manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which boosts market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of instances when INDPRO crosses above 100, or the threshold signal fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
